nodes.label: Does skin color affect educational and labor market outcomes in the United States?
Colorism / skin-color stratification and intergenerational transmission of skin-color advantage
nodes.rationale: Uses standard regression and sibling fixed-effects models to distinguish a direct 'skin color premium' from inherited advantages due to skin-color stratification in prior generations; finds no schooling effect net of family SES but a positive income effect of lighter skin for Black men and women.
